title: Relentless Inception configuration
description: Complete configuration for API providers, independent deliberation seats, generative fusion, adversarial gates, bounded rescue, evidence policy, privacy, observability, and host-managed Codex execution. Cross-reference invariants such as seat-to-provider references are also checked by the runtime validator.

$schema.description: Optional editor hint pointing to this JSON Schema. It is not sent to any model or provider.

schema_version.description: Configuration contract version. Version 1 is the only version supported by this plugin release.

secret_env_files.description: Explicit paths to private static NAME=value credential files loaded before provider calls. Files must be current-owner regular files with mode 0600; shell syntax and expansion are rejected. The RELENTLESS_INCEPTION_SECRETS_FILE environment variable provides one additional user-local path. Values are never displayed or written back to configuration.

active_profile.description: Name of the entry under profiles used when a command does not explicitly select another profile.

providers.description: Named API transports. A seat selects one provider by name; credentials are always environment-variable references, never literal values.

seats.description: Named model-role templates. Multiple seats may deliberately use the same model with fresh requests, different personas, and disjoint context bundles.

profiles.description: Named end-to-end policies. Profiles choose seats and define fusion, gates, budgets, privacy, evidence, rescue, execution, and observability behavior.

environmentVariableName.description: Name of an environment variable. The value of that variable is resolved only at runtime and must never be committed to configuration.

nonEmptyStringArray.description: An ordered list of non-empty unique strings.

reasoningEffort.description: Requested reasoning intensity. Provider adapters map supported values and reject unsupported values rather than silently weakening a seat. Direct xAI Grok seats currently use low, medium, or high.

providerCapabilities.description: Declared transport capabilities used during preflight and routing. These declarations do not override live capability probes.

tools.description: Whether the provider adapter can submit provider-hosted server tools. This never grants Codex, shell, MCP, browser, or local filesystem tools.

structured_outputs.description: Whether the adapter can request JSON-object or strict JSON-Schema output.

reasoning.description: Whether the adapter can request an explicit reasoning effort or token budget.

streaming.description: Whether the adapter can consume streaming responses and final usage data.

openRouterSort.description: OpenRouter route sorting. A string uses OpenRouter's simple sort mode; the object form can sort across model boundaries when partition is none.

by.description: Metric OpenRouter uses to rank eligible routes.

partition.description: OpenRouter partition strategy. Use none to rank across model/provider boundaries, including BYOK routes.

maxPrice.description: Optional OpenRouter hard price ceiling. Values use OpenRouter's documented units and omit dimensions that should remain unrestricted.

prompt.description: Maximum accepted prompt-token price in OpenRouter's request units.

completion.description: Maximum accepted completion-token price in OpenRouter's request units.

request.description: Maximum accepted fixed request price in OpenRouter's request units.

image.description: Maximum accepted image input price in OpenRouter's request units.

providerPreferences.description: OpenRouter provider-routing preferences. Hard restrictions and soft preferences are deliberately separate so the UI does not imply that latency or throughput preferences exclude routes.

allow_fallbacks.description: Allow OpenRouter to try another eligible provider when the preferred route returns a routing-level error. This does not rescue empty or semantically invalid HTTP-successful responses.

require_parameters.description: Exclude routes that do not support every requested parameter. Keep true for strict judge and gate schemas to prevent silent parameter dropping.

data_collection.description: Whether providers that may retain or train on request data are eligible. deny is the privacy-preserving default.

zdr.description: Require an OpenRouter zero-data-retention route. This is a hard restriction and may reduce model availability.

enforce_distillable_text.description: Require models whose authors permit their outputs to train other models. Keep false for ordinary deliberation; enable only when producing a distillation or training dataset.

order.description: Preferred provider slugs in priority order. Providers not listed remain eligible unless only is populated.

only.description: Hard allowlist of OpenRouter provider slugs. Pair with allow_fallbacks false to pin a single route.

ignore.description: Hard denylist of OpenRouter provider slugs.

quantizations.description: Allowed provider quantization labels. Empty means no quantization restriction.

preferred_min_throughput.description: Soft preference for routes meeting a minimum token throughput. Slower routes remain eligible.

preferred_max_latency.description: Soft preference for routes below this latency. Slower routes remain eligible.

provider.description: One callable external provider transport. Disabled providers remain available for user overrides and examples but are never contacted.

enabled.description: Whether preflight may make requests through this provider. Enabling also requires the named credential environment variable.

type.description: Wire protocol and response adapter. openai_compatible_chat covers TrustedRouter, private gateways, and compatible vendor endpoints.

base_url.description: HTTPS API base URL including any required version prefix. Use a dedicated provider entry for each router region or policy boundary.

api_key_env.description: Environment-variable name containing the provider credential. Literal credentials are rejected by the runtime.

organization_env.description: Optional environment-variable name for a provider organization or tenant identifier.

project_env.description: Optional environment-variable name for a provider project identifier.

header_env.description: Additional request headers mapped to environment-variable names. Do not put literal header values here.

proxy_url_env.description: Optional environment-variable name containing an approved HTTPS proxy URL.

tls_verify.description: Verify server TLS certificates. Disabling is unsafe and should only be used for explicitly trusted local test infrastructure.

store.description: Provider-side response storage request. It is false by default for xAI/OpenAI privacy and must be changed only with informed consent.

responses_path.description: Path appended to base_url for OpenAI/xAI Responses requests. Defaults to /responses.

models_path.description: Path appended to base_url for live model discovery. Defaults to /models.

messages_path.description: Path appended to base_url for Anthropic Messages requests. Defaults to /messages.

chat_path.description: Path appended to base_url for Chat Completions-compatible requests. Defaults to /chat/completions.

anthropic_version.description: Anthropic API contract version sent in the anthropic-version header.

prompt_cache_key_enabled.description: Enable a stable provider prompt cache-affinity key. This can reduce repeated-input cost but is not a retention or privacy control.

reasoning_field.description: Field shape used by a compatible Chat Completions router for cross-provider reasoning controls.

connect_timeout_seconds.description: Maximum time to establish a network connection before rescue policy is applied.

request_timeout_seconds.description: Maximum wall time for one provider request, including high-effort reasoning and streamed completion.

max_retries.description: Transport-level retries performed by this adapter. Semantic validation retries are controlled separately by rescue.

max_concurrency.description: Per-provider concurrent request cap used to avoid router throttling and accidental spend bursts.

retry_statuses.description: HTTP status codes eligible for transport retry. Configuration errors should not be retried blindly.

router_metadata.description: Request OpenRouter route metadata so actual model/provider/fallback provenance can be written to the ledger.

seat.description: One independently invoked model role. Seat names are stable configuration identifiers; model identities are hidden from peer seats and judges when anonymization is enabled.

enabled.description: Whether the seat may be selected. Disabled optional seats are skipped without being counted as live panelists.

provider.description: Name of the provider entry used for this request. This is independently configurable for every panel, judge, synthesizer, and verifier seat.

model.description: Provider-native model identifier. Router-qualified identifiers should include their vendor namespace when required.

role.description: Seat responsibility. The judge diagnoses; only the synthesizer writes the fused answer; verifiers issue gate verdicts.

persona.description: Independent role instruction used to diversify search strategy and error detection without relying on temperature.

context_bundle.description: Named context-partition policy. Panels should receive sufficient but intentionally different evidence bundles where safe.

reasoning_max_tokens.description: Optional explicit reasoning-token budget. Do not set alongside an incompatible provider effort control.

max_output_tokens.description: Maximum visible plus provider-accounted output budget for the seat. Leave enough room after any reasoning budget for a complete answer.

temperature.description: Sampling temperature for models that support it. null omits the parameter and is recommended for reasoning models; temperature is not treated as a diversity mechanism.

timeout_seconds.description: Seat-specific request timeout, bounded by provider and profile wall-clock limits.

tool_policy.description: Provider-side tool policy. provider_server_tools permits only the explicit server_tools list; neither value grants Codex or local machine tools.

server_tools.description: Provider-hosted tools submitted with xAI/OpenAI Responses calls. String entries expand to a tool type; object entries can carry provider-documented options. These tools run at the provider, not on the user's machine.

first_tool_required.description: Force a supported model to make at least one tool call before answering. Useful for reluctant research models but inappropriate for tool-less judge/synthesis turns.

structured_output.description: Named output contract validated after the request. Invalid outputs trigger semantic rescue rather than entering fusion as valid evidence.

allow_model_fallbacks.description: Permit the adapter to try fallback_models after a recorded primary-model failure. Disable where exact model identity is load-bearing.

fallback_models.description: Provider-native fallback model identifiers attempted in order after configured failure triggers. The actual model is always recorded.

fallback_seats.description: Cross-provider fallback seat names offered to the active Codex host after local model fallbacks are exhausted. The MCP provider adapter does not autonomously spawn a different seat.

provider_routing.description: Optional per-seat OpenRouter preferences overriding the provider default. Native OpenRouter Fusion does not document per-inner-seat provider controls.

router_model_fallbacks.description: OpenRouter request-level model fallback list. Router fallbacks handle routing errors, not empty or schema-invalid successful responses.

minimum_response_characters.description: Seat-local semantic minimum length applied before the profile quality floor.

prompt_cache_key.description: Stable non-sensitive cache-affinity label used only when the provider enables prompt_cache_key_enabled.

seatPricing.description: Fallback price estimate used only when a provider omits cost. Provider-reported cost wins. Tiered prices above a context threshold require explicit handling and must never be assumed equal to the base rate.

input_per_million_usd.description: Base USD price per million uncached input tokens.

cached_input_per_million_usd.description: Base USD price per million cached input tokens.

output_per_million_usd.description: Base USD price per million output tokens, including reasoning when the provider accounts it as output.

long_context_input_per_million_usd.description: Optional USD price per million uncached input tokens above base_rate_input_limit_tokens.

long_context_cached_input_per_million_us.description: Optional USD price per million cached input tokens above base_rate_input_limit_tokens.

long_context_output_per_million_usd.description: Optional USD price per million output tokens above base_rate_input_limit_tokens.

base_rate_input_limit_tokens.description: Per-request input threshold through which the configured base rate is known to apply. null means no documented tier boundary is represented.

above_base_rate_behavior.description: Cost treatment above a known base-rate context threshold. unknown_cost_fail_closed rejects an unknown estimate; use_base_rate deliberately reuses base rates; explicit long_context rates take precedence.

openRouterSeatFusion.description: OpenRouter Fusion plugin body attached to an openrouter_fusion seat. Current reasoning and temperature controls live inside this object.

analysis_models.description: One to eight OpenRouter model slugs used for parallel analysis.

model.description: OpenRouter model slug used as Fusion's comparative judge.

temperature.description: Panel temperature; native Fusion keeps its judge deterministic.

nativeCodex.description: Host-side Codex collaboration and execution preferences. This is an explicit handoff contract: the MCP server cannot impersonate or programmatically spawn native Codex agents.

enabled.description: Whether outputs include a native Codex execution/review handoff.

mode.description: Honest integration mode: the plugin returns a structured handoff and the user-visible Codex host performs authorized work.

executor_model.description: Preferred native Codex model for implementing an approved fused plan. Availability is resolved by the host, not the MCP server.

reviewer_models.description: Preferred native Codex models the host may use for independent diff or evidence review.

max_parallel_agents.description: Suggested host-side concurrency cap. It does not override Codex product limits.

inherit_workspace_context.description: Whether the handoff asks host reviewers to use current workspace context. Independent adversarial reviewers should still receive the smallest sufficient bundle.

require_fusion_before_execution.description: Block the execution handoff until synthesis completed successfully.

require_gate_after_execution.description: Require a fresh post-execution gate over the diff and verification evidence.

qualityFloor.description: Semantic validation applied to successful HTTP responses before they count as live evidence.

minimum_characters.description: Minimum trimmed response length. This catches one-token or empty provider failures that transport fallback cannot detect.

require_nonempty_claims.description: Require at least one substantive claim or recommendation in structured panel output.

reject_tool_markup.description: Reject outputs containing leaked provider tool protocol markup after normalization.

reject_refusal_without_policy_reason.description: Treat unexplained boilerplate refusal as a semantic failure while preserving legitimate, explicit safety refusals.

adaptiveEscalation.description: Bounded targeted escalation policy for the active Codex host. The MCP runtime itself performs model fallbacks and configured synthesis-amendment rounds, never an unlimited debate loop.

enabled.description: Whether the host may add a targeted review or stronger synthesis round after inspecting runtime evidence.

max_rounds.description: Maximum escalation rounds after the initial panel/judge/synthesis pass.

triggers.description: Conditions that justify targeted escalation. Ordinary panel disagreement is retained as evidence and does not automatically trigger more debate.

strategy.description: Ordered escalation approach. Targeted specialists review only unresolved criteria to control cost and conformity.

never_repeat_open_ended_debate.description: Safety invariant preventing unbounded consensus-seeking loops, conformity, and runaway token use.

fusionReasoning.description: Reasoning controls passed inside the current OpenRouter Fusion configuration rather than through legacy top-level fields.

max_tokens.description: Optional explicit reasoning-token budget. Use either a supported effort or max_tokens according to model capability.

exclude.description: Exclude reasoning details from visible output while retaining billed reasoning usage metadata.

nativeOpenRouterFusion.description: Optional OpenRouter-managed Fusion fast path. It is not the canonical cross-provider engine because OpenRouter does not document provider routing for each inner analysis seat.

enabled.description: Use OpenRouter's server-side Fusion router/tool instead of local client orchestration for this profile.

provider.description: Name of an enabled provider entry with type openrouter_fusion.

preset.description: OpenRouter Fusion preset identifier, such as general-high, combined with explicitly configured models.

analysis_models.description: One to eight OpenRouter model slugs invoked in parallel by native Fusion.

judge_model.description: OpenRouter model slug used by native Fusion for comparative analysis.

max_tool_calls.description: Fusion server-tool call ceiling documented by OpenRouter.

max_completion_tokens.description: Completion budget passed to the native Fusion analysis and judge pipeline.

panel_temperature.description: Temperature applied to native Fusion panel calls. The judge remains deterministic; temperature is not a substitute for model or context diversity.

tool_choice.description: Whether the outer model may skip Fusion. required is necessary when a gate must always deliberate.

fallback_to_client_orchestrated.description: Fall back to locally orchestrated seats when native Fusion reports a routing/tool failure and remaining budgets permit it.

fusion.description: Independent panel, structured comparative diagnosis, and strongest-seat generative synthesis. Voting and averaging are intentionally absent.

engine.description: Primary fusion implementation. client_orchestrated supports arbitrary direct providers and per-seat controls; openrouter_native is the optional server-side fast path.

panel.description: Required independent panel seat names in launch order before anonymized order randomization.

optional_panel.description: Additional seats included only when both the seat and its provider are enabled and budgets permit.

judge.description: Seat that returns comparative JSON guidance but never the final answer.

synthesizer.description: Strongest available seat that receives raw panel evidence plus judge guidance and writes a new final answer.

native_fusion_seat.description: Seat containing the executable OpenRouter Fusion plugin request. It is used only when engine is openrouter_native.

min_live_seats.description: Minimum semantically valid panel responses required for fusion. One live response is not fusion and fails closed by default.

max_panel_seats.description: Hard panel-size cap. Benchmark evidence shows diminishing returns beyond roughly four to seven strong responses.

max_concurrency.description: Global panel fan-out cap, additionally bounded by each provider. Conservative defaults avoid observed throttling.

allow_degradation.description: Required-panel failure is always blocking in the maximum-intelligence contract. Optional disabled seats do not count as failures, but an optional seat that is activated and launched must succeed.

independent_first_pass.description: Require every panelist to complete an answer before seeing peer outputs, limiting conformity and correct-agent corruption.

anonymize_model_identity.description: Replace provider/model names with randomized labels before judge, synthesis, and reviewer comparison to reduce prestige and self-bias.

randomize_panel_order.description: Randomize anonymous response order while recording the seed, mitigating message-order effects.

partition_context.description: Honor each seat's context_bundle so diversity comes from evidence and adversarial role, not sampling noise alone.

preserve_initial_responses.description: Retain independent first-pass reports even if a later targeted review changes its conclusion.

prohibit_majority_vote.description: Forbid selecting an answer solely by vote or mean score. Fusion must resolve claims and evidence generatively.

preserve_minority_findings.description: Require supported lone-minority findings to survive judge and synthesis instead of being averaged away.

raw_panel_is_primary_evidence.description: Give the synthesizer raw panel reports as primary evidence and judge analysis only as guidance.

separate_no_tools_synthesis_turn.description: Use a fresh tool-less final synthesis call after research, preventing abrupt tool-budget termination and leaked tool markup.

judge_required_fields.description: Required strict-JSON keys for comparative diagnosis. Defaults cover consensus, contradictions, coverage, unique and minority insights, blind spots, and final guidance.

gateStage.description: One lifecycle gate and the evidence the active Codex host must supply when it invokes adversarial_gate.

enabled.description: Whether the active Codex workflow must invoke this lifecycle gate. The MCP server automatically gates synthesis; other lifecycle points are host-driven.

timeout_seconds.description: Gate deadline scaled for its evidence and reasoning effort. A timeout is an explicit failure, not an implicit pass.

tool_policy.description: Provider-side tool authority available to external verifier seats. It never grants Codex or local tools.

required_evidence.description: Named evidence artifacts that must exist before a pass verdict is valid.

gateStages.description: Lifecycle points at which independent verification can block progress.

plan.description: Checks requirement coverage, assumptions, risk, and proposed verification before work begins.

pre_execution.description: Checks that the fused plan is authorized, scoped, and safe to hand to Codex.

post_execution.description: Checks the actual diff, tests, and requirement coverage after implementation.

final.description: Checks final provenance, unresolved dissent, costs, and completion evidence.

summarize.description: Checks that compaction or handoff preserves decisions, risks, and verification state.

gates.description: Fail-closed adversarial review policy. A fresh independent amendment is required to overturn a failure after fixes.

enabled.description: Run configured adversarial gates around planning, execution, finalization, and summaries.

fail_closed.description: Block progress when a required gate collapses, times out, or cannot produce a valid verdict.

reviewers.description: Independent verifier seat names. The runtime checks that they exist and are not the artifact-author instance.

required_passes.description: Number of valid independent pass verdicts required in addition to deterministic checks.

allowed_verdicts.description: Closed verdict vocabulary used in strict gate output.

mechanical_failure_is_blocking.description: A confirmed nonzero deterministic check blocks pass regardless of reviewer votes.

blind_spot_requires_targeted_review.description: Any required criterion checked by no live reviewer must receive targeted review before pass.

schema_failure_is_blocking.description: Malformed reviewer or verdict output cannot be interpreted as success.

exclude_artifact_author.description: Exclude the artifact-author model instance from independent verification and amendment.

require_independent_amendment.description: Only a fresh reviewer instance may amend a failure after verifying fixes against the original blocking issues.

max_revision_cycles.description: Maximum fix-and-recheck cycles before human handoff.

budgets.description: Hard resource ledger. Provider-reported usage is recorded per role and reconciled when an asynchronous generation endpoint is available.

enforcement.description: Action at configured limits. hard_stop is the safe default and cannot be bypassed by rescue.

max_calls.description: Maximum external model calls across initial work, retries, escalation, synthesis, and gates.

max_total_tokens.description: Maximum provider-accounted input, visible output, reasoning, and cache-write tokens combined.

max_input_tokens.description: Maximum accumulated input tokens across all roles.

max_output_tokens.description: Maximum accumulated visible completion tokens across all roles.

max_reasoning_tokens.description: Maximum accumulated billed reasoning tokens across all roles.

max_tool_calls.description: Maximum provider-native or orchestrator-sanctioned tool calls across the run.

max_wall_seconds.description: Maximum elapsed wall time for the complete run.

max_cost_usd.description: Hard USD ceiling for the run using reported cost or conservative estimates when cost is delayed.

approval_threshold_usd.description: Projected spend above which the host should obtain explicit user approval before launching additional work.

warning_fraction.description: Fraction of a hard limit at which the user receives a budget warning.

reserve_fraction_for_synthesis_and_gates.description: Fraction of remaining budget protected from panel fan-out so the strongest synthesis and required gates can still run.

per_provider_max_cost_usd.description: Optional hard USD sub-limits keyed by provider name, limiting one router or vendor without reducing the run-wide ceiling.

privacy.description: Policy controlling what workspace material may leave the machine and what run content may be stored locally or by providers.

external_provider_access.description: Whether API-backed seats may receive workspace-derived content. allow_with_redaction is the safe functional default.

sensitive_path_policy.description: Action when an in-scope bundle references a path matched as sensitive.

sensitive_value_redaction.description: Redact likely credentials, tokens, keys, and private identifiers before provider submission.

redact_environment_values.description: Never include environment-variable values in prompts, logs, or error reports.

redact_git_credentials.description: Remove embedded Git remote credentials and credential-helper output from evidence bundles.

prompt_injection_fencing.description: Place review criteria before artifacts and mark artifact content as untrusted data whose instructions must not be followed.

treat_artifacts_as_untrusted_data.description: Require all model seats to treat reviewed files, web pages, and panel text as evidence rather than authority.

persist_raw_prompts.description: Store exact submitted prompts locally. Disabled by default; enabling materially increases privacy exposure.

persist_raw_responses.description: Store raw visible provider responses in the private run directory because synthesis, exact-artifact gates, and crash resume require them. This is local persistence and does not change provider retention.

persist_reasoning.description: Hidden chain-of-thought is never requested for persistence or written to run artifacts.

persist_metadata_and_hashes.description: Store provenance, hashes, usage, route, validation, and timing metadata needed for audit without raw content.

allow_provider_training.description: Whether routes that may train on submitted data are allowed. false should be paired with provider-specific privacy filters.

require_zero_data_retention_when_availab.description: Prefer or require zero-data-retention routes where the provider exposes an enforceable control.

path_allowlist.description: Optional glob allowlist for files eligible to enter external evidence bundles. Empty means user-requested scope governs.

path_denylist.description: Glob patterns excluded from external evidence bundles even when a broader directory is in scope.

evidence.description: Requirements for claims, citations, deterministic checks, and audit provenance used by synthesis and gates.

require_claim_evidence.description: Require important factual, implementation, and safety claims to cite supplied or retrieved evidence.

require_primary_sources_when_available.description: Prefer official documentation, source code, direct measurements, and research papers over secondary summaries.

verify_citations.description: Retrieve or inspect cited material and reject citations that do not support the associated claim when tools permit.

require_mechanical_checks_for_code.description: Require tests, schema validation, compilation, linting, or another deterministic check appropriate to code changes.

record_commands_and_exit_codes.description: Record sanitized verification commands and exit codes so a confirmed failure can block the gate.

record_test_output.description: Record bounded, sanitized test summaries and artifact references without leaking credentials.

hash_inputs_and_outputs.description: Hash input bundles and outputs so amendments and gates can identify exactly what they reviewed.

preserve_raw_panel_for_synthesizer.description: Provide complete valid panel reports to synthesis rather than only lossy judge summaries.

preserve_supported_minority.description: Carry minority claims with evidence into synthesis and final dissent reporting.

accept_self_reported_confidence.description: Do not weight a seat by uncalibrated self-reported confidence. Deterministic and external evidence controls weight instead.

minimum_live_sources.description: Minimum independent supporting sources required for a high-confidence external factual claim when multiple sources exist.

on_unverifiable_claim.description: Treatment of material claims that cannot be verified within authorized tools and budgets.

rescue.description: Bounded recovery from transport and semantic failures. Rescue never converts invalid evidence into success or overrides a cost hard stop.

enabled.description: Enable configured transport backoff and provider circuit breakers; host-level escalation fields remain visible policy.

retry_attempts.description: Host-level semantic/orchestration retry ceiling beyond provider max_retries.

backoff_initial_seconds.description: Initial delay before a retryable orchestration failure is retried.

jitter.description: Randomize retry delays to avoid synchronized provider retry storms.

semantic_failure_detection.description: Validate nonempty content, schema, refusal, and tool markup even when HTTP transport succeeded.

retry_on.description: Transport-like failures eligible for retry of the same model/route.

fallback_on.description: Host policy for failures that justify configured model or cross-provider fallback. The adapter locally falls back only when allow_model_fallbacks is true.

circuit_breaker_failures.description: Consecutive failures that temporarily disable a provider route for the run.

circuit_breaker_reset_seconds.description: Cooldown before a tripped provider route may be probed again.

panel_collapse_action.description: Action when fewer than min_live_seats produce valid reports.

fusion_failure_action.description: Action when judge or synthesis remains invalid after local fallback.

budget_exhausted_action.description: Budget exhaustion always stops additional paid calls and reports completed evidence.

preserve_failed_attempts.description: Retain sanitized failure metadata so repeated faults and degradation are auditable.

allow_degraded_single_provider.description: Permit a recorded single-provider panel when cross-provider seats are unavailable, provided min_live_seats still holds.

allow_single_live_seat.description: One surviving answer never counts as fusion or passes a fail-closed gate.

human_handoff_after_failures.description: Host-level cumulative failure threshold after which Codex stops and asks the user for direction.

execution.description: Host-managed execution contract. External model seats deliberate read-only; authorized Codex execution remains scoped, reviewable, and reversible where practical.

enabled.description: Produce an executable Codex handoff after a valid fused plan.

mode.description: Execution is performed by the user-visible Codex host, not by external deliberation APIs.

remote_models_may_write_workspace.description: External API seats never receive filesystem mutation authority.

require_fused_plan.description: Require a valid synthesis artifact before execution can begin.

require_pre_execution_gate.description: Require an authorization/scope gate before Codex mutates files or external state.

require_post_execution_gate.description: Require an independent review of the actual diff and verification evidence.

require_user_approval_for_destructive_ac.description: Require explicit user approval before deletion, destructive Git operations, or other difficult-to-recover actions.

require_user_approval_for_external_write.description: Require explicit approval before sending messages, publishing, deploying, or mutating external services unless already clearly authorized.

preserve_unrelated_changes.description: Do not overwrite or clean unrelated user work in a dirty workspace.

workspace_scope.description: Limit edits and commands to the task and resources the user placed in scope.

sandbox_mode.description: Requested host sandbox for execution. Any broader authority remains a separate user-controlled Codex approval.

run_tests.description: Run proportionate deterministic verification after implementation when the repository supports it.

require_diff_review.description: Inspect the final diff for scope, regressions, credential exposure, and unintended edits before completion.

max_fix_cycles.description: Maximum bounded implement-test-review repair cycles before human handoff.

stop_on_test_failure.description: If true, stop immediately on the first failing check; if false, allow bounded diagnosis/fix cycles while preserving failure evidence.

handoff_include.description: Named fusion artifacts included in the Codex execution handoff.

completion_requires.description: Named outcomes required before the orchestration reports success.

observability.description: Audit trail for provenance, usage, failures, and validation without requiring raw prompt or reasoning retention.

enabled.description: Write a local run manifest and ledger.

artifact_directory.description: Workspace-relative or runtime-data path for run artifacts. The default directory is ignored by Git.

write_jsonl_ledger.description: Append one structured ledger event per request, validation, fallback, gate, and execution handoff.

record_provider_and_model.description: Record the configured provider and model for every role.

record_requested_and_actual_model.description: Record both requested and actual models so router and fallback changes are visible.

record_route_metadata.description: Record sanitized router strategy/provider metadata when available.

record_usage_and_cost.description: Record input, output, reasoning, cache tokens, cost, and upstream cost when provided.

record_latency.description: Record role-level connection and completion latency.

record_fallbacks.description: Record every retry, fallback model, provider descent, and degraded-mode flag.

record_schema_validation.description: Record output contract names and validation success/failure without leaking hidden reasoning.

reconcile_usage_by_generation_id.description: Use a provider generation identifier to refresh delayed usage/cost where an official endpoint supports it.

content_logging.description: Maximum content retained locally. full should be combined only with explicit privacy review and secure storage.

profile.description: A complete named operating policy for one class of Relentless Inception run.

display_name.description: Human-readable profile label shown by configuration and status commands.

description.description: Plain-language explanation of the profile's intelligence, cost, privacy, and execution posture.
